---
title: "Literature Evidence Register"
---

## Scope

Four literature bodies are reviewed:

1. **Proprioceptive training interventions** — do balance and foot-pressure interventions produce 
   lasting changes in movement patterns? What outcome measures are standard?
2. **Golf weight transfer and GRF** — what is the baseline evidence on pressure 
   shift patterns and their measurement?
3. **Foot pressure intervention in golf** — has anyone directly manipulated foot 
   pressure during the swing and measured the effect?
4. **Golf training aid efficacy trials** — peer-reviewed before/after studies 
   on golf training interventions

## Extraction Template

Each paper is extracted using the following structure:

```
## [Author(s), Year] — Short title

**Source**: Journal / Conference  
**DOI/URL**:  
**Study type**: RCT / Observational / Case study / Review / Meta-analysis  
**Population**: n, skill level, age, sex  
**Intervention**: What was done  
**Comparator**: Control condition  
**Duration**: Training period  
**Outcome measures**: What was measured and how  
**Key findings**: Main results  
**Relevance to trial design**: What this tells us about our protocol  
**Limitations noted by authors**:  
**Quality flag**: High / Medium / Low confidence  
```

---

## Area 1: Proprioceptive Training Interventions

### Identified papers (relevance pre-check)

| Paper | Relevance | Status |
|---|---|---|
| Eils & Rosenbaum (2001) — proprioceptive training ankle sprain | High — RCT with clear outcome measures | To extract |
| Hupperets et al. (2009) — home proprioceptive training ankle sprain RCT (BMJ) | High — 35% reduction in recurrence, good design | To extract |
| Zech et al. (2010) — proprioceptive training sport systematic review | High — outcome measure summary across sports | To extract |
| Benis et al. (2024) — proprioceptive training sports performance systematic review (PMC11225257) | High — covers multiple sports, performance outcomes | To extract |
| TOGU training golf swing 4 weeks (PMC11587031) | High — directly relevant, golf + balance training + before/after | **Priority extract** |
| ACL proprioception RCT (Tandfonline 2025) | Medium — outcome measures useful but not golf | To extract |

---

## Area 2: Golf Weight Transfer and GRF

### Identified papers (relevance pre-check)

| Paper | Relevance | Status |
|---|---|---|
| Ground Reaction Force and COP systematic review (Sports Medicine 2026) | **Critical** — most recent systematic review on GRF/COP in golf | **Priority extract** |
| Foot-ground interaction and clubhead speed (Frontiers 2026) | High — impulse-based energy transfer framework | To extract |
| Foot insole pressure distribution professionals vs amateurs (MDPI 2022) | High — directly relevant measurement methodology | To extract |
| Blenkinsop et al. (2018) — uphill/downhill slopes weight transfer (JAB) | Medium — COP shift data, good methodology reference | To extract |
| Wallace & Graham (1990) — foot pressure golf drive case study | Medium — historical baseline, first WSCG | Background |
| Richards — weight transfer patterns skilled vs unskilled | High — directly cited in multiple papers | To find and extract |

---

## Area 3: Foot Pressure Intervention in Golf

### Identified papers (relevance pre-check)

| Paper | Relevance | Status |
|---|---|---|
| Alterations in foot CoP weight distribution lower body kinematics (Roehampton/J Sports Sci 2019) | **Critical** — closest existing study to our intervention | **Priority extract** |
| Ground-X-Factor proprioceptive foot intervention preprint (Preprints.org 2024) | **Critical** — immediate effect of foot proprioceptive intervention on GRF and club speed, n=25 | **Priority extract** |
| Latella et al. (2008) WSCG — golf specific proprioception program | High — before/after design, swing mechanics outcomes | To extract |

---

## Area 4: Golf Training Aid Efficacy Trials

### Identified papers (relevance pre-check)

| Paper | Relevance | Status |
|---|---|---|
| Cochran & Farrally (eds) Science and Golf series — training aid chapters | Medium — background, not RCTs | Background |
| Evidence for biomechanics and motor learning improving golf performance (ResearchGate 2012) | High — review of what actually works | To extract |
| SuperSpeed Golf GRF study (Standifird, Utah Valley) — before/after vertical force | High — example of training aid RCT design | To find full paper |

### Preliminary finding

No peer-reviewed RCT of a foot-pressure or balance-training intervention specifically 
for golf weight transfer was identified. This represents the primary gap this project 
addresses.

---

## Full Extractions

*To be added as papers are processed*

---

## Key Themes Emerging

*To be populated after extractions*

---

## TODOs

- [ ] Extract TOGU golf study (PMC11587031) — most directly comparable design
- [ ] Extract GRF systematic review (Sports Medicine 2026) — outcome measure reference
- [ ] Extract Roehampton foot CoP intervention study — closest methodological precedent
- [ ] Extract Ground-X-Factor preprint — most recent foot proprioception intervention
- [ ] Find Richards weight transfer patterns paper full text
- [ ] Find Standifird SuperSpeed GRF study full text
